Name:	Blake, Humfrey, gent.
Dates:	1559
Place:	Ouerstowey, Somerset, England
Book:	Burialls (Burial)
Collection:	Somerset: - Calendar of Wills, 1537-1799, and Administration in the Court of the Archdeacon of Taunton
Volume:	Calendar of Wills and Administrations in the Court of the Archdeacon of Taunton.
Chapter:	Part 1. Wills in Registers 1537-1593.
Text:	Blake, Humfrey, gent., Ouerstowey 1559 3 25

zh.lifeSummary: 當Humphrey Blake於大約1494年出生在英國哈普爾安多福時，他的父親William Blake30歲，母親Mary Coles26歲。他於1519年6月2日與Agnes在英國薩默塞特奧維 斯度維結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他在英國英格蘭奧可福爾奧可福德被列為education。他去世於1558年12月28日在英國薩默塞特奧維 斯度維享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特奧維 斯度維。

nameMeanings.name: Blake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (England and central Scotland): variant of Black 1, meaning ‘swarthy’ or ‘dark-haired’, from a byform of the Old English adjective blæc, blac ‘black’, with change of vowel length.

English: nickname from Middle English blak(e) (Old English blāc) ‘wan, pale, white, fair’. In Middle English the two words blac and blāc, with opposite meanings, fell together as Middle English blake. In the absence of independent evidence as to whether the person referred to was dark or fair, it is now impossible to tell which sense was originally meant.
English (Norfolk): nickname from Middle English bleik, blaik>, blek(e) (Old Norse bleikr) ‘pale or sallow’ (in complexion).

variants.name: Bleak
meanings: English: mostly a variant of   Blake  , but perhaps also of   Blick  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Black
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Blaikie
meanings: Scottish: nickname from a pet form of   Blake  .

variants.name: Blakes
meanings: English: variant of Blake , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Humphrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

variants.name: Humphry
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

variants.name: Humfry
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Humphrey Blake was born about 1494, in Andover, Hampshire, England, his father, William Blake, was 30 and his mother, Mary Coles, was 26. He married Agnes on 2 June 1519, in Over Stowey, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. His occupation is listed as education in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. He died on 28 December 1558, in Over Stowey, Somerset, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in Over Stowey, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

Sa profession de son indiquée est education à Oxford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 28 décembre 1558, à Over Stowey, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Over Stowey, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Humphrey Blake(이)가 대략 1494년 영국 함프실르 앤도버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Blake(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Coles(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1519년 6월 2일 영국 서머세트 버 스타우이에서 Agnes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스퍼드에서 education이었다. 그는 1558년 12월 28일 영국 서머세트 버 스타우이에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 버 스타우이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Humphrey Blake родился около 1494 г. в местности андовир, хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Blake было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Coles, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes 2 июня 1519 г. в местности овир стоуи, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери.  Он работал по профессии education в местности оксфорд, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 28 декабря 1558 г. в местности овир стоуи, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности овир стоуи, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.
